text: The Future of Venture Capital: Unlocking Liquidity and Growth The liquidity engine is broken. ~1,920 unicorns sit privately held at $7.3T post-money, ~$3T trapped as unrealized NAV in VC funds, time-to-IPO has stretched to ~12 years, and US venture funds have drawn $196.9B more from LPs than they've returned since 2022. Secondaries hit $106B in 2025 (~30% of US exit value) but 86.4% of volume is concentrated in just 20 names Liquidity exists for OpenAI/SpaceX/Stripe shareholders, not for the long tail US + China hold ~72% of unicorn count and ~82% of unicorn value, with 4 companies (OpenAI, ByteDance, SpaceX, Anthropic) worth more than every European + Indian unicorn combined. Conversion rates are brutal outside the top two: 1-in-60 VC-backed firms become unicorns in North America/Asia vs. 1-in-135 in Europe and 1-in-330 in sub-Saharan Africa AI is rewriting the unit economics and breaking the asset-class boundaries. AI took >50% of global VC deal value in 2025; five companies (OpenAI, Scale, Anthropic, Prometheus, xAI) absorbed $84B alone (~20% of global VC). AI-native firms hit $100M ARR in under a year with tiny teams, which kills traditional ARR-based valuation frameworks (functionality is replicable in weeks, moats erode)
author: admin#1742094092

text: The Agent's Legal Body: How AI Agents Get the Right to Contract AI agents excel at contracts and analysis but remain legal tools without personhood, unable to own assets, sign independently, or litigate They require four primitives: own, contract, litigate, and persist - granted routinely to corporations via LLCs Bayern's zero-member LLC wraps agents in legal shells: operating agreements delegate governance to algorithms, enabling memberless existence

text: Highlight Clip Bybit CEO Ben: I Don't Buy Companies Blaming Layoffs on AI In an April 23 interview with When Shift Happens, Bybit CEO Ben Zhou said many companies may simply want to cut jobs and use AI as an excuse, but he does not yet believe AI can replace a large share of the workforce. Zhou said the real challenge today is measuring AI output: internal spending on large-model tokens is rising quickly, but the actual efficiency gains are hard to quantify. In his view, AI is more like when companies first adopted Excel - a major change, but how much it will transform each department still needs to be tested. — link

text: how to enter side doors - a field guide to jobs, cold emails, and making yourself legible to the right people Jobs aren't objects on job boards - they're bundles of problems someone wants solved. The front door (job applications) is saturated and lossy: hundreds of AI-polished resumes per role, weak signal extraction on the employer's side. Side doors work better because they generate different signal A company is a group of humans with bottlenecks - some scoped into job posts, many sitting as unsolved tension at the edges. "Getting a job" means finding someone with a problem and convincing them you can solve it. Once you see this, the question shifts from "what's posted?" to "whose work do I care about, where are they stuck, what could I show up with?"

text: The AI Infrastructure Boom Has Created a Massive Memory Shortage Massive AI demand and roughly $1T in capex have triggered severe bottlenecks for both DRAM and NAND memory chips This unprecedented demand has allowed historically commoditized memory-makers to experience skyrocketing operating income and massive pricing power, which is expected to pass down a 10-20% price increase to consumer devices like PCs and phones

text: From Open Source Software to Open Source Strategy How the Smartest Executives Are Using Open Source Techniques to Optimize Corporate Strategy Open source as corporate weapon: No longer just a dev methodology - companies use it to neutralize dominant rivals, kill supplier pricing power, and force industry standards (Android vs. Apple, Kubernetes vs. AWS) The Geopolitical and AI Stakes: The battle between open and closed models is actively playing out in artificial intelligence and autonomous vehicles. Because leading U.S. labs are keeping their frontier AI models closed and Meta has stepped back from open releases, China has embraced open-weight models as a national strategy to leapfrog Western tech leadership. If Western regulation restricts open competition, Chinese models risk becoming the default standard for the rest of the world

text: Anthropic | 2028: Two Scenarios for Global AI Leadership A paper on the AI competition between the US and China. Anthropic argues that democracies, led by the US currently hold a critical compute advantage over China's AI ecosystem, but that advantage is being eroded through chip smuggling, overseas data center loopholes, and large-scale distillation attacks on US frontier models. The paper presents two 2028 scenarios: > One where the US locks in a 12-24 month lead by closing export control loopholes and defending its model innovations. > The second is where inaction allows the Chinese Communist Party (CCP) to reach near-parity, enabling AI-powered authoritarianism and undermining democratic norms globally. text: Mullvad VPN doesn't pick your outgoing (exit) IP address randomly every time you connect. Instead, it uses a fixed, predictable math formula based on your WireGuard connection key Because of a quirk in how their random number generator works, the IPs you get on different servers always follow very similar patterns The researcher tested thousands of keys and found only 284 unique IP combinations reeee - creates an easy "fingerprint" that can link your different connections or accounts together
author: admin#1742094092

text: Notes on AI, labor, and China China observations • China already has structural urban youth unemployment, pre-AI, caused by mass college enrollment skewing supply toward knowledge work, weak household consumption, and policy favoring state firms • Coping pathways: tangping (lying flat, niche), care economy + "full-time children" living off boomer savings, brutal gig work (delivery at ~$14/day), and the "iron rice bowl" SOE/government jobs that function as de facto jobs programs (overstaffed parks, subways, etc.) • Despite robotics firms openly pitching human replacement, AI-jobs panic is muted because: (1) competition was already cutthroat, (2) workers default to "techno-determinist pragmatism" - master AI or lose, no room for conscientious objection, (3) cheaper human labor makes automation less ROI-positive (a 3,000 RMB job isn't worth replacing), and (4) the state intervenes - a Beijing arbitration ruling held that firing someone because AI can do their job violates the Labor Contract Law
author: admin#1742094092

text: Highlight Clip Eric Trump: We are the most debanked family in the world Eric Trump stated at the Consensus conference that his family faced a comprehensive ban from the traditional banking sector, becoming the "most debanked family in the world." He believes the traditional financial system is broken and punitive toward consumers, citing that banks pay depositors only 10 basis points in interest while taking a 4% spread to build luxury skyscrapers. Eric believes that cryptocurrency can eliminate exorbitant intermediary fees and democratize finance, while also serving as a decentralized store of value immune to political interference and natural disasters. — link

Dunamu operates Upbit, South Korea's largest crypto exchange. — link

text: Binance Research said illicit crypto transactions account for less than 1% of total on-chain transaction volume. As of 2025, more than $75 billion in illicit funds remained on-chain, up about 28% from 2024. Major mixers have limited daily processing capacity, meaning that even laundering $1 billion in stolen funds could take more than 100 days. In addition, over 80% of illicit on-chain funds have been moved to downstream addresses, but blockchain ledgers still allow fund flows to be continuously traced. — link
author: admin#1534592031
